meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

source_tables.name: 14-10-0416-01 Labour force characteristics by occupation, annual

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Middle management occupations in production and agriculture
family.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
family.definition: This major group comprises middle management occupations in production and agriculture. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 0. The occupations classified under TEER 0, as those included in this group with zero (0) as the second digit of their code, are considered management occupations.
family.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
family.major_group_title: Middle management occupations in production and agriculture
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

units.title: Managers in natural resources production and fishing
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.major_group_title: Middle management occupations in production and agriculture
units.sub_major_group_title: Middle management occupations in production and agriculture
units.minor_group_title: Managers in natural resources production and fishing
units.definition: Managers in natural resources production and fishing plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the operations of establishments in forestry and logging, mining and quarrying, oil and gas drilling, production and servicing operations, and commercial fishing.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Managers in natural resources production and fishing
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Managers in natural resources production and fishing
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Managers in natural resources production and fishing plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the operations of establishments in forestry and logging, mining and quarrying, oil and gas drilling, production and servicing operations, and commercial fishing.
employment_requirements: Oil and gas managers usually require a bachelor's degree in geology, earth sciences or petroleum engineering.
Forestry managers usually require a bachelor's degree in forestry science or forest engineering.
Mining and quarrying managers usually require a bachelor's degree in mining engineering or earth sciences.
Several years of experience in a supervisory occupation in the particular industry are usually required and may substitute for formal education requirements.
additional_information: Progression to senior management positions in the respective industries is possible with experience.

example_titles.job_title_text: oil and gas fields production manager

core_competencies.statement: to secure beneficial agreements regarding mining, forestry, drilling or fishing and maintain smooth business relationships with suppliers, clients and partners
to organize the replacement of equipment and material requirements of work to minimize waste and reduces costs
to clearly communicate with field workers, regulatory agencies or stakeholders, ensuring safety procedures are followed, operational goals are understood and instructions are accurately conveyed
to organize operational and production information from forestry, logging, mining and quarrying for reports to senior management
to guide teams, make critical decisions, resolve conflicts, ensure compliance with regulations and adapt to industry changes
to understand operational strategies to develop plans that drive long-term success in resource extraction and meet production quotas

units.title: Managers in agriculture
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.major_group_title: Middle management occupations in production and agriculture
units.sub_major_group_title: Middle management occupations in production and agriculture
units.minor_group_title: Managers in agriculture, horticulture and aquaculture
units.definition: Managers in agriculture plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the operations and functions of farms. They are responsible for growing crops, raising and breeding livestock, poultry and other animals, marketing farm products and enhancing farms' sustainability through, notably, the implementation of new technologies. They usually own and operate their own establishment.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Managers in agriculture plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the operations and functions of farms. They are responsible for growing crops, raising and breeding livestock, poultry and other animals, marketing farm products and enhancing farms’ sustainability through, notably, the implementation of new technologies.
employment_requirements: Extensive farming experience, obtained as a farm supervisor or specialized crop or livestock worker or by working on a farm, is usually required.
A university degree or college diploma in agricultural management or other field related to crop or livestock production may be required.
additional_information: Managers in agriculture manage farms of various sizes which may specialize in particular crops such as wheat, apples or potatoes or raise particular livestock such as beef cattle, swine or poultry.

core_competencies.statement: to adapt strategies and modify operations in response to unpredictable factors like weather conditions, market fluctuations or disease outbreaks
to manage equipment and product needs, such as seeds, fertilizers and feed, to minimize waste and reduces costs
to identify pest infestations, soil deficiencies or other issues and determine the causes to implement effective solutions
to generate and implement new techniques and technologies to improve crop yields, livestock health and farm efficiency
to ensure smooth operations, fosters motivation and promotes sustainable farming practices, enabling the farm to thrive both operationally and environmentally while achieving long-term success
to understand best practices, emerging technologies and industry challenges to develop operational plans to ensure farm performance and sustainability

units.title: Managers in horticulture
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.major_group_title: Middle management occupations in production and agriculture
units.sub_major_group_title: Middle management occupations in production and agriculture
units.minor_group_title: Managers in agriculture, horticulture and aquaculture
units.definition: Managers in horticulture plan, organize, direct and control the activities of nursery and greenhouse staff who grow and market trees, shrubs, flowers and plants.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Managers in horticulture plan, organize, direct and control the activities of nursery and greenhouse staff who grow and market trees, shrubs, flowers and plants.
employment_requirements: Completion of a college program in horticulture is usually required.
Experience as a nursery or greenhouse supervisor is required.

core_competencies.statement: to optimize operations and address challenges promptly to ensure the overall success of horticultural projects
to ensure resources are stocked and used effectively, such as soil, fertilizers, irrigation systems or greenhouse equipment
to aid in introducing new plant varieties, enhancing growth techniques and responding to market trends effectively
to diagnose plant diseases, nutrient deficiencies or environmental problems and implement appropriate solutions to prevent potential damage to crops
to manage staff efficiently, ensuring tasks such as planting, maintenance and customer service are performed effectively
to apply best practices, manage plant health effectively and make informed decisions that drive long-term success

units.title: Managers in aquaculture
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.major_group_title: Middle management occupations in production and agriculture
units.sub_major_group_title: Middle management occupations in production and agriculture
units.minor_group_title: Managers in agriculture, horticulture and aquaculture
units.definition: Managers in aquaculture manage operations of facilities which cultivate and harvest fish, shellfish or marine plants for replenishment of wildlife stocks or for commercial sale. They are employed by public or private fish hatcheries and commercial aquatic farms, or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Managers in aquaculture manage operations of facilities which cultivate and harvest fish, shellfish or marine plants for replenishment of wildlife stocks or for commercial sale.
employment_requirements: A commercial aquaculture or fish hatchery licence, permit or lease is required for self-employed aquaculture managers.
Completion of secondary school is usually required.
A college diploma in aquaculture or a related field orseveral years of experience in fishing or aquaculture operations are usually required.

core_competencies.statement: to optimize operations and address challenges promptly to ensure the overall success of aquaculture projects and environments
to ensure resources are stocked and used effectively, such as feed, water treatment products and equipment
to adopt innovative practices regarding breeding, feeding or managing aquatic species to improve growth rates, sustainability and efficiency
to identify disease outbreaks, water quality issues or feed deficiencies to swiftly address these issues and minimize impact on aquatic stocks
to inspire trust and commitment in their staff to implement strategies, handle unforeseen challenges and maintain high productivity

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Livestock, Farm Animals & Wildlife
core_competencies.statement: to provide appropriate care, optimize conditions for growth and ensure the health and productivity of fish, shellfish or marine plants
